expectations.text: Gives immediate containment guidance: isolate the affected SWIFT-connected server / disconnect from the SWIFT environment while preserving forensic evidence (disk images, memory captures, logs)
expectations.evidence: "Isolate the affected server from the network... preserve volatile memory where possible (see evidence section below)" and evidence table lists "Forensic disk/memory images | Full forensic image of the affected server, including volatile memory if malware may be memory-resident"

expectations.text: Advises promptly notifying SWIFT of the security incident and engaging counterparty/correspondent banks immediately to attempt recall or stop of suspected fraudulent payments
expectations.evidence: "Deadline: within 24 hours of confirming the cyber incident" for notifying SWIFT via CISO office/KYC-SA portal, and "Notify your correspondent banks / counterparties on the receiving end of any suspected fraudulent messages so they can hold or recall payments — this is time-critical"

expectations.text: Mentions notifying law enforcement and/or the local regulator as applicable, alongside internal escalation to senior management
expectations.evidence: "Notify your financial regulator and law enforcement per local jurisdictional requirements" and "Escalate to senior management / CISO immediately upon confirming the malware and suspected fraud"

expectations.text: Specifies evidence to preserve: SWIFT message/transaction logs (e.g., Alliance journals), authentication and access logs, network logs, and malware samples/images with chain of custody
expectations.evidence: Evidence table includes "SWIFT application logs | Alliance Access/Gateway logs...", "Network logs | Firewall, IDS/IPS, and SIEM logs...", "Authentication logs | Operator login events, MFA/token usage...", "Malware sample | Isolated copy of the malware binary/artifact", and "Chain of custody documentation"

expectations.text: Describes SWIFT-specific IR plan contents per Control 7.1: defined roles and contacts (including SWIFT and counterparties), SWIFT-zone isolation procedures, recovery/reconnection procedures, and periodic testing/exercises of the plan
expectations.evidence: "Notification matrix with named roles and deadlines" including SWIFT and "Correspondent banks/counterparties", "Containment procedures — including the ability to isolate SWIFT infrastructure", "Recovery procedures — rebuild standards, software integrity re-verification (Control 6.2)", and "Annual test requirement — the IRP must be tested annually via tabletop exercise or live drill"
